entries.title: Adapter (C++ design pattern)
entries.summary: Convert the interface of a class into another interface the clients expect.

entries.summary: Decouple an abstraction from its implementation so that the two can vary independently.

entries.summary: Compose objects into tree structures to represent part-whole hierarchies.

entries.summary: Attach additional responsibilities to an object dynamically. Decorators

entries.summary: Provide a unified interface to a set of interfaces in a subsystem. Facade

entries.summary: Define an interface for creating an object, but let subclasses decide which class to instantiate.

entries.title: Proxy (C++ design pattern)
entries.summary: Proxy pattern provides a surrogate or placeholder for another object to control access to it.

entries.title: Singleton (C++ design pattern)
entries.summary: Ensure a class only has one instance, and provide a global point of access to it.

entries.summary: The pattern allows an object to alter its behavior when its internal state changes.
